source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In experiments on the reaction $^{21}\text{Ne}(\text{d, }^{3}\text{He}){}^{20}\text{F}$ with 26 MeV deuterons, many states in $^{20}\text{F}$ are excited. The angular distributions are characteristic of the direct reaction mechanism and therefore are easily sorted into those for which the angular momentum of the transferred proton is $l_{p}=0$ or 1 or 2. The lowest energy levels of $^{21}\text{Ne}$ and the known negative‐parity states of $^{20}\text{F}$ below 4 MeV are as shown in Fig. 2.41 (the many positive‐parity excited states of $^{20}\text{F}$ are omitted). The relative $S\text{(}J^{\text{π}}\text{)}$ observed in the (d, $^{3}\text{He}$) reaction are approximately: $S\text{(}1^{-}\text{)}=0.84$, $S\text{(}2_{1}^{-}\text{)}=0.78$, $S\text{(}2_{2}^{-}\text{)}=0.79$, $S\text{(}3^{-}\text{)}=0.00$. In the limit of weak coupling; i.e., with no residual interaction between the hole and the particles, what would be the (relative) energies of the 4 negative‐parity states?
extraction_notes: `images` contains a PIL image object string representation, not a file path or URL.

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: In experiments on the reaction $^{21}\text{Ne}(\text{d, }^{3}\text{He}){}^{20}\text{F}$ with 26 MeV deuterons, many states in $^{20}\text{F}$ are excited. The angular distributions are characteristic of the direct reaction mechanism and therefore are easily sorted into those for which the angular momentum of the transferred proton is $l_{p}=0$ or 1 or 2. The lowest energy levels of $^{21}\text{Ne}$ and the known negative‐parity states of $^{20}\text{F}$ below 4 MeV are as shown in Fig. 2.41 (the many positive‐parity excited states of $^{20}\text{F}$ are omitted). The relative $S\text{(}J^{\text{π}}\text{)}$ observed in the (d, $^{3}\text{He}$) reaction are approximately: $S\text{(}1^{-}\text{)}=0.84$, $S\text{(}2_{1}^{-}\text{)}=0.78$, $S\text{(}2_{2}^{-}\text{)}=0.79$, $S\text{(}3^{-}\text{)}=0.00$. In the limit of weak coupling; i.e., with no residual interaction between the hole and the particles, what would be the (relative) energies of the 4 negative‐parity states?
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: `images` contains a PIL image object string representation, not a file path or URL.
metadata.reasoning_chain: We have $H=H_{p}+H_{h}+V_{p h}$, where $H_{p}$ and $H_{h}$ are respectively the Hamiltonian of the nuclear center and the hole, and $V_{p h}$ is the potential due to the interaction of the hole and the nuclear center. In the limit of weak coupling, $V_{p h}=0$. Then for the four negative-parity states we have $E_{3^{-}}=E_{2_{1}^{-}}, E_{2_{2}^{-}}=E_{1^{-}}$, as shown in Fig. 2.42, with values $E_{3^{-}}=E_{2_{1}^{-}}=1230 \text{keV}, E_{2_{2}^{-}}=E_{1^{-}}=890 \text{keV}$.

The angular distributions are characteristic of the direct reaction mechanism and therefore are easily sorted into those for which the angular momentum of the transferred proton is $l_{p}=0$ or 1 or 2. The lowest energy levels of $^{21}\text{Ne}$ and the known negative‐parity states of $^{20}\text{F}$ below 4 MeV are as shown in Fig. 2.41 (the many positive‐parity excited states of $^{20}\text{F}$ are omitted). The angular distributions are characteristic of the direct reaction mechanism and therefore are easily sorted into those for which the angular momentum of the transferred proton is $l_{p}=0$ or 1 or 2. The lowest energy levels of $^{21}\text{Ne}$ and the known negative‐parity states of $^{20}\text{F}$ below 4 MeV are as shown in Fig. 2.41 (the many positive‐parity excited states of $^{20}\text{F}$ are omitted). The relative $S\text{(}J^{\text{π}}\text{)}$ observed in the (d, $^{3}\text{He}$) reaction are approximately: $S\text{(}1^{-}\text{)}=0.84$, $S\text{(}2_{1}^{-}\text{)}=0.78$, $S\text{(}2_{2}^{-}\text{)}=0.79$, $S\text{(}3^{-}\text{)}=0.00$. In the limit of weak coupling; i.e., with no residual interaction between the hole and the particles, what would be the (relative) energies of the 4 negative‐parity states?
